seoTitle: How to View the Backlog in Jira
metaDescription: Learn how to find and navigate the backlog view in Jira so you can see all unscheduled issues waiting to be planned into a sprint.
intro: The backlog is where all the work that hasn't been scheduled into a sprint lives, and knowing how to navigate it makes sprint planning much smoother. This guide covers finding and using the backlog view in Jira.

steps.heading: Open your project
steps.body: Navigate to the Jira project that has the backlog you want to view.

steps.heading: Select the backlog view
steps.body: Find the backlog option in the project's side navigation, separate from the active sprint board.

steps.heading: Review unscheduled issues
steps.body: Scroll through the list of issues that haven't yet been assigned to a sprint.

steps.heading: Prioritize the list
steps.body: Drag and drop issues to reorder them by priority, since order in the backlog typically reflects what should be tackled first.

steps.heading: Move issues into a sprint
steps.body: Select issues from the backlog and add them to an upcoming sprint when you're ready to plan.

faq.question: Is the backlog only available for Scrum projects?
faq.answer: Backlog views are primarily a feature of Scrum-style boards; Kanban boards typically use a continuous flow view instead, though a backlog can sometimes be enabled.

faq.question: Can I filter the backlog to see specific types of issues?
faq.answer: Yes, you can apply filters and quick filters to the backlog view to narrow it down by assignee, type, or other criteria.

faq.question: How do I know which issues are highest priority in the backlog?
faq.answer: Priority is generally reflected by an issue's position in the list, with items higher up meant to be tackled sooner.

faq.question: Can I add new issues directly from the backlog view?
faq.answer: Yes, most Jira backlog views let you create a new issue directly without leaving the page.
